steps.title: Use the spectrum evidence
steps.content: Silicon cells capture the red range of light, while perovskite captures the blue range. Silicon alone converts 18-22% of sunlight, and perovskite alone is no more efficient, but combining the two raises conversion to 29.5% or more.

steps.title: Explain the combined effect
steps.content: B contradicts the statement that perovskite-only cells are no more efficient than silicon ones. C claims solar power needs replacement technology, which the passage does not support. D shifts to other minerals, which are not discussed. The combination works because it captures more of the solar spectrum. The answer is A.
